implementation_summary: Closed explicit production gaps: upload route now schedules the ingestion worker after new upload commit, document upload/list/status/delete routes are public for local reviewer use without IAM/JWT gating, /chat returns structured citation metadata, Chainlit calls /api/v1/chat and renders answer-level Chicago citation notes, Next.js root is an updated public chat workspace, and Playwright e2e scaffold covers upload-to-indexed-to-Chainlit cited-answer smoke using a generated table PDF fixture.
divergences_logged: ARCHITECTURE (4).md section 18 records FastAPI background-task upload handoff, backend-owned citation metadata, and runtime-generated Playwright PDF fixture decisions. Real gold score floors remain trust-gated until corpus fetch/checksum pinning, indexing, and human expected-answer verification.
verification_requirements: Targeted verification complete: npm test --prefix frontend -- --runInBand passed; docker compose -p assessment exec backend pytest app/tests/test_auth.py app/tests/test_documents.py -vv passed 9 tests after rebuild; live GET /api/v1/documents returned 200 with no token and with invalid bearer header; no-file POST /api/v1/documents returned 422 instead of 401; backend health returned ok. Playwright live smoke, clean-clone dry run, and full backend/frontend verification remain pending.

chat_surface_decision: BOTH. Next.js :3000 and Chainlit :8000 are supported, equivalent chat surfaces over the same /api/v1/chat contract, which the starter explicitly allows ('in place of or alongside'). Neither client owns retrieval, generation, or citation logic, so a behavioral difference between them is a bug, not a feature. Logged in ARCHITECTURE (4).md section 18.
implementation_summary: Added a backend response-presentation boundary that owns every writing-style and citation rule: it validates [cite:n] markers against backend citation candidates, places Chicago superscripts at the end of the sentence each supports, drops invalid markers, strips filename/document-name openings and internal details, and assembles the reference list from chunk metadata only so the model can never invent a source or page. Answers claiming facts without a surviving citation become a concise no-answer rather than being shown ungrounded, and only grounded cited answers are cache-eligible. Migration 0014 stores source_chunk_ids on both caches so a cache hit rebuilds the reference list its superscripts point at. Rebuilt both chat surfaces: Next.js gained an app shell with route-driven active nav, a hamburger drawer at <=1024px, a loading row with duplicate-send prevention, inert-text answer rendering with linked superscripts and a Sources list, and a + upload button at every viewport; Chainlit gained a loading placeholder replaced in place, mirrored Sources rendering, Starter cards, and a + Upload PDF button via its documented [[UI.header_links]] component. All user-facing copy was rewritten in plain language with jargon guards in tests.
defects_found_and_fixed: DeterministicGenerationClient opened every answer with 'Based on <filename>.pdf, ...' - the exact anti-pattern the reviewer reported.

Off-corpus questions returned a confident, correctly-cited answer about the wrong topic: retrieval's nearest neighbour was always quoted back, and lexical grounding could not catch it because the sentence really was verbatim from a source. Generation now requires a multi-word question to match a source on more than one content word.
Cached answers kept their superscripts but lost their citations, rendering a dangling marker with no Sources list.
The mobile menu scrim was exposed as a second button carrying the same label as the toggle, duplicating the close control in the accessibility tree.
A hand-rolled Chainlit floating upload button collided with Chainlit's own header link at 1440px. Caught by screenshots, not by assertions; replaced with the documented component.
A user whose PDF was still processing was told to 'upload a document first'. Preparing and empty-corpus are now distinct messages.
divergences_logged: ARCHITECTURE (4).md section 18 records the both-surfaces decision, the response-presentation boundary, the [cite:n] citation contract, no-answer conversion for uncited claims, cache source_chunk_ids, and the Chainlit documented-component decision. Section 21 now documents corpus/input, answer-behavior, and deployment assumptions.
verification_requirements: Verified: backend pytest -> 161 passed, 12 skipped, 4 warnings in 62.95s; RAG system integration suite -> 17 passed; npm test --prefix frontend -> 21 passed; tsc --noEmit clean; python3 -m unittest chainlit_app.tests.test_chat -> 10 passed; npx playwright test e2e/chat-ui.spec.ts -> 16 passed in real Chromium covering both chat surfaces at 375x812, 768x1024, 1024x768, and 1440x900; both localhost:3000 and localhost:8000 checked live and screenshotted; backend health ok. Still pending: the upload-to-cited-answer live ingestion e2e, a clean-clone dry run, and real gold score runs (corpus fetch/checksum pinning/indexing/human expected-answer verification).
